subtitle: Semantic Web Applications and Tools for Health Care and Life Sciences
hosted_by: Department of Medical Informatics, Amsterdam UMC
Advanced Compute & Data Core (ACDC), Amsterdam UMC

sessions.title: Clinical Data Modeling with SULO
sessions.abstract: Hands-on introduction to clinical data modeling using the Semantic Unified Language for Ontologies (SULO). Participants will learn to represent clinical knowledge using formal ontological patterns.

sessions.title: Making GraphRAG work for Healthcare and Life Sciences: Ensuring Knowledge Graph Quality for Grounded LLMs
sessions.abstract: Practical workshop on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using knowledge graphs in healthcare and life sciences. Focus on knowledge graph quality criteria that ensure reliable LLM grounding.

sessions.title: Building Virtual Knowledge Graphs for Life Sciences with Ontop-VKG
sessions.abstract: Learn to construct and query virtual knowledge graphs over relational and other data sources in life sciences using Ontop, without physically transforming the data.

sessions.title: Harmonising Provenance Information for Biomedical Data: the MIRAPIE Approach Applied to Health Research Data in EHDS Context
sessions.abstract: Practical session on provenance harmonization for biomedical datasets using the MIRAPIE framework, applied to the European Health Data Space (EHDS) context.

sessions.title: Declarative Data Standardization to OMOP-CDM using LinkML
sessions.abstract: Learn to map heterogeneous health datasets to the OMOP Common Data Model using declarative LinkML schemas, reducing manual transformation effort.

sessions.title: Learning SPARQL By Example
sessions.abstract: Beginner-friendly introduction to SPARQL for querying RDF knowledge graphs in the life sciences and healthcare, with hands-on examples on real biomedical datasets.

sessions.title: Looking in and through the looking-glass
sessions.speaker: Prof. Ronald Cornet
sessions.abstract: Reflects on 25 years researching semantics and ontologies for healthcare data representation, addressing technical, social, and governance aspects while examining progress, pitfalls, and future directions in semantic interoperability for reusable health data.
sessions.bio: Full Professor of Medical Informatics at Amsterdam UMC. His work focuses on semantic interoperability and information modeling, with consistent emphasis on making clinical and biomedical data more FAIR.

sessions.title: Scaling up data driven research can leverage the EHDS but needs better quality and more semantically interoperable data
sessions.speaker: Prof. Dipak Kalra
sessions.affil: European Institute for Innovation through Health Data (i~HD)
sessions.abstract: Examines how the European Health Data Space accelerates real-world data availability while highlighting critical success factors including data provenance, quality, standards, and public trust.
sessions.bio: President of i~HD. Champions approaches combining technical interoperability with ethical, legal, and organisational trust to support responsible data sharing across healthcare and research.

sessions.title: Poster & Demo Session
sessions.abstract: Presentations of all accepted posters and demonstrations. Coffee served.

sessions.title: Conference Dinner
sessions.abstract: Social dinner included with registration. A great opportunity to connect with fellow attendees over food and drinks.

sessions.title: Knowledge alignment for LLMs: A strategy for reproducibility?
sessions.speaker: Prof. Janna Hastings
sessions.abstract: Discusses combining large language models with formal ontologies and logical constraints to improve reliability and ensure reproducibility, with examples from chemistry and behavioural science.
sessions.bio: Previously at EBI and University of Cambridge. From January 2026, leads the Human-Centred Health AI group at Idiap, focusing on improving AI approaches for evidence and decision-making.

sessions.title: Keynote — Prof. Hannah Bast
sessions.speaker: Prof. Hannah Bast
sessions.abstract: Talk title to be announced. Prof. Bast specialises in algorithms, information retrieval, and high-performance systems for knowledge graph search, bridging rigorous computer science with real-world applications.
sessions.bio: Professor at University of Freiburg specialising in algorithms, information retrieval, and high-performance systems for RDF and SPARQL use cases.

sessions.title: Poster & Demo Session
sessions.abstract: Second poster and demo session. Coffee served.

sessions.title: Project Pitches & Team Formation
sessions.abstract: Each project lead presents their pitch (5 min). After all pitches, teams self-organise and hacking begins.

sessions.title: Results Presentations & Wrap-up
sessions.abstract: Each team presents their hackathon outcomes, code, and next steps.

talks.title: Scholia 2026: Compliance with SPARQL 1.1
talks.authors: Egon Willighagen, Daniel Mietchen, Peter Patel-Schneider, Konrad Linden, Lars Willighagen, Wolfgang Fahl

LLM-based agents for term reconciliation
talks.authors: Iurii Savvateev, Taras Günther, Martin Wainaina, Matthias Filter

talks.title: Flyover: A Practical, Privacy-Aware Tool for Clinical Data FAIR-ification and Semantic Harmonization
talks.authors: Varsha Gouthamchand, Joshi Hogenboom, Tim Hendriks, Andre Dekker, Leonard Wee, Johan van Soest

talks.title: Machine-Actionable Access Policies using ODRL
talks.authors: Jens van der Wateren, Rick Overkleeft, Sander van Boom

talks.title: Proposal2VR: Turning a Consortium Funding Proposal into an Ontology-Grounded RDF Graph for Immersive Exploration in Virtual Reality
talks.authors: Alexander Kellmann, Kirubel Biruk Shiferaw, Daniele Liprandi, Dagmar Waltemath, Ron Henkel

talks.title: RDFlow: monitoring data interoperability and ontology evolution for rare diseases
talks.authors: Pauline Lubet, Jose-Emilio Labra-Gayo, Núria Queralt-Rosinach, Andra Waagmeester, Daniel Fernández-Álvarez, Yasunori Yamamoto, Xi Yang

talks.title: KIK-V health indicator data exchange: Linked Data from the ground up
Sequential Domain Adaptation on Heterogeneous Clinical Resources for Biomedical NER
Semantic Mapping of TREAT-NMD Core Datasets to the CARE-SM
talks.authors: Lilli Schuckert, Pablo Alarcón-Moreno, Daphne Wijnbergen, Sander van Boom, Bouchra Ezzamouri, Marco Roos, Ronald Cornet, Martijn G. Kersloot

talks.title: FDPcrawleR: A Lightweight R Framework for Auditing FAIR Data Points and FAIR Virtual Platforms
talks.authors: Kristina Vodorezova, Alberto Cámara, Nirupama Benis, Andra Waagmeester, Mark D. Wilkinson, Ronald Cornet

talks.title: Ontological modeling of dynamic biodiversity consensus
Automated Quality Metrics Assessment in Real World Data for Rare Diseases Registries EU Regulatory Compliance – AutoQ-RWD
talks.authors: Sergi Aguió-Castillo, Bruna dos Santos Vieira, Inês de Oliveira Coelho Henriques, Peter A. C. ’t Hoen

talks.title: STELA: Unifying SNOMED CT Logical Expressions and Textual Descriptions for Holistic Concept Representation
talks.authors: Yuanyuan Zheng, Adel Bensahla, Julien Ehrsam, Jamil Zaghir, Christian Lovis, Christophe Gaudet-Blavignac, Mina Bjelogrlic

talks.title: Embedding-based Deduplication of Knowledge Graphs using Graph Neural Networks
talks.authors: Emma Pinckers, Yulia Shapovalova, Shervin Mehryar, Michel Dumontier

talks.title: Semantic Interoperability at National Scale: The SPHN Federated Clinical Routine Dataset
talks.authors: Jan Armida, Vasundra Touré, Philip Krauss, Deepak Unni, Harald Witte, Davide Chiarugi, and co-authors

talks.title: A Standards-Based Knowledge Graph that Bridges Scientific Workflows, Run-Time Provenance, and Tool Registries
talks.authors: Marie Schmit, Ulysse Le Clanche, George Marchment, Sarah Cohen-Boulakia, Olivier Dameron, Alban Gaignard, Frédéric Lemoine, Hervé Ménager

biohackathon.note: Parallel hacking room available March 23–25. Main event March 26.

projects.title: From DCAT to HealthDCAT-AP
projects.description: Comparative analysis of healthcare metadata catalogue schemas across Europe. Participants will analyse similarities and differences among national DCAT-AP and HealthDCAT-AP implementations to create harmonisation recommendations.

projects.title: OpenMRS as a Living Lab
projects.leads: Claude Nanjo, Stephanie Medlock, Ronald Cornet, Andra Waagmeester (Amsterdam UMC)
projects.description: Uses open-source OpenMRS to demonstrate integration with Semantic Web knowledge sources for enriching clinical data with linked biomedical knowledge, focusing on privacy-preserving approaches and reproducible patterns for decision support.

projects.title: Bring Your Concepts
projects.leads: Pedro Paulo F. Barcelos et al. (Health-RI)
projects.description: Hands-on mapping clinic aligning concepts from ontologies and vocabularies to a shared semantic hub, operationalising the Health-RI Semantic Interoperability Initiative framework.

projects.title: Microscopy Metadata Meets Semantic Web
projects.description: Focuses on the intersection of Semantic Web technologies and the microscopy ecosystem, including OMERO platform, OME-NGFF standard, and bioimaging repositories for strengthened interoperability.

travel_alert: ⚠️ Extensive road and rail maintenance is scheduled throughout 2026 in Amsterdam. Check live travel conditions before setting off.

from_anywhere.label: From Schiphol Airport
steps: Take NS Sprinter (platform -1) direct to Amsterdam Holendrecht — no changes needed.
Journey time: ~25 min. Single ticket from €5.50 (OVpay: just tap your bank card).
Exit Holendrecht station, cross the station square and walk over the bridge — the AMC main entrance is visible directly ahead. (~5 min walk)

from_anywhere.label: From Amsterdam Centraal
steps: Metro line 50 (direction Gein or Gaasperplas) from Amsterdam Centraal → exit at Amsterdam Holendrecht. Journey ~35 min.
OR: NS Sprinter from Amsterdam Centraal to Amsterdam Holendrecht. Journey ~28 min.
Exit station, cross the square and walk over the bridge to the AMC main entrance. (~5 min walk)

from_anywhere.label: By bus
steps: Bus lines 120, 276, 370, 41, 47 and night bus N85 all stop near AmsterdamUMC, location AMC.
Nearest stops: Holendrechtplein, Amsterdam Meibergdreef, Tafelbergweg.
Use 9292.nl or Moovit to plan from your starting point.

from_anywhere.label: By car
steps: Signposted from Amsterdam ring road A10 (exit S112), and from A2 and A9.
⚠️ Allow extra travel time — extensive road maintenance throughout 2026. Check Waze or Google Maps for live conditions.

from_anywhere.label: By bicycle
steps: Cycle via the new park along Meibergdreef.
Free covered bike parking available beneath the stairs opposite the main entrance.
Use CyclOSM for a cycling-optimised map.

indoor_nav.title: Inside AmsterdamUMC, location AMC
indoor_nav.address_full:
AmsterdamUMC, location AMC
Meibergdreef 9
1105 AZ Amsterdam-Zuidoost
Netherlands
indoor_nav.conference_rooms: TBA — room assignments will be posted here and on the interactive programme
steps_from_entrance: Enter via the Main Entrance at Meibergdreef 9 (directly opposite the bridge from Holendrecht station).
Follow SWAT4HCLS signage inside the building.
Registration desk: TBA (will be updated before the conference).
Ask at the hospital information desk if in doubt — staff speak English.
tips: The AMC is a large hospital campus — allow 5 extra minutes to find your room on Day 1.
Toilets and a canteen are available throughout the building.
Accessibility: the building is fully wheelchair accessible. Contact swat4hcls@gmail.com for specific needs.

transport.label: Nearest station
transport.detail: Amsterdam Holendrecht — served by Metro 50, Metro 54, and NS Sprinter. Exit station, cross the square and walk over the bridge (~5 min) — the AMC main entrance is visible directly ahead.

transport.label: From Amsterdam Centraal
transport.detail: Metro 50 (direction Gein/Gaasperplas) → Holendrecht (~35 min). Or NS Sprinter → Holendrecht (~28 min).

transport.label: From Schiphol Airport
transport.detail: NS Sprinter direct to Amsterdam Holendrecht (~25 min, from €5.50 OVpay). Walk 5 min to AMC entrance.

transport.label: By bicycle
transport.detail: Via the park along Meibergdreef. Free covered bike parking opposite the main entrance.

transport.label: By car
transport.detail: Signposted from A10 (exit S112), A2, and A9. Paid parking on site. ⚠️ Allow extra time — 2026 road works.

tickets.name: OVpay (contactless card/phone)
tickets.note: Tap in/out with bank card. Daily GVB cap €10.50.
